message:
Split lists/records guide and add sort/slice examples (#225)

* Split lists/records guide and add sort/slice examples

Split the combined "Shape lists and records" guide into two focused pages:
- shape-lists.mdx: lists guide with new sort (desc, cmp), slice, and
  sort-and-slice sections
- shape-records.mdx: records guide (access, keys, merge, transform, filter)

Also update the sort reference page (.md → .mdx) with See Also links,
add sort/shape-lists cross-references to slice, and update all 16 files
that referenced the old combined guide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename sort-and-slice section to top-k and bottom-k

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TQL examples: remove set operator, fix output bugs

- Replace `set` operator with plain assignment throughout both guides
- Fix distinct() output ordering by chaining .sort()
- Fix verified output for "example.com" (ends_with(".example.com") is false)
- Fix deprecated map(u, {...}) syntax to map(u => {...})

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add set semantics section with add() and remove() examples

Cover add() for set-insertion and remove() for element deletion in the
lists guide. Add reciprocal See Also links from both reference pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1,25 +1,23 @@
 ---
-title: Shape lists and records
+title: Shape lists
 ---
 
-Lists and records are fundamental data structures in TQL. This guide shows you
-how to work with these collections - accessing elements, transforming values,
-and combining data structures.
-
-## Work with lists
+import Fn from "@components/see-also/Fn.astro";
+import Guide from "@components/see-also/Guide.astro";
 
-Lists (arrays) contain ordered sequences of values. Let's explore how to
-manipulate them.
+Lists (arrays) contain ordered sequences of values. This guide shows you how to
+work with lists — accessing elements, sorting and slicing, transforming values,
+and combining data structures.
 
-### Access list elements
+## Access list elements
 
 Get values from specific positions:
 
 ```tql
 from {items: ["first", "second", "third", "fourth"]}
-set first_item = items[0]
-set last_item = items[-1]
-set length = items.length()
+first_item = items[0]
+last_item = items[-1]
+length = items.length()
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -37,15 +35,15 @@ Index notation:
 - `[-1]` - Last element (negative indices count from end)
 - `.length()` - Get the number of elements
 
-### Add elements to lists
+## Add elements to lists
 
 Use [`append()`](/reference/functions/append) and [`prepend()`](/reference/functions/prepend):
 
 ```tql
 from {colors: ["red", "green"]}
-set with_blue = colors.append("blue")
-set with_yellow = with_blue.prepend("yellow")
-set multi_append = colors.append("blue").append("purple")
+with_blue = colors.append("blue")
+with_yellow = with_blue.prepend("yellow")
+multi_append = colors.append("blue").append("purple")
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -57,7 +55,7 @@ set multi_append = colors.append("blue").append("purple")
 }
 ```
 
-### Combine lists
+## Combine lists
 
 Join multiple lists with [`concatenate()`](/reference/functions/concatenate) or spread syntax:
 
@@ -67,9 +65,9 @@ from {
   list2: [4, 5, 6],
   list3: [7, 8, 9]
 }
-set combined = concatenate(concatenate(list1, list2), list3)
-set spread = [...list1, ...list2, ...list3]
-set with_value = [...list1, 10, ...list2]
+combined = concatenate(concatenate(list1, list2), list3)
+spread = [...list1, ...list2, ...list3]
+with_value = [...list1, 10, ...list2]
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -83,7 +81,7 @@ set with_value = [...list1, 10, ...list2]
 }
 ```
 
-### Transform list elements
+## Transform list elements
 
 Apply functions to each element with [`map()`](/reference/functions/map):
 
@@ -92,9 +90,9 @@ from {
   prices: [10, 20, 30],
   names: ["alice", "bob", "charlie"]
 }
-set with_tax = prices.map(p => p * 1.1)
-set uppercase = names.map(n => n.to_upper())
-set squared = prices.map(x => x * x)
+with_tax = prices.map(p => p * 1.1)

 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -107,7 +105,7 @@ set squared = prices.map(x => x * x)
 }
 ```
 
-### Filter list elements
+## Filter list elements
 
 Keep only elements that match a condition with [`where()`](/reference/functions/where):
 
@@ -118,9 +116,9 @@ from {
 }
 // Note: The modulo operator (%) is not currently supported in TQL
 // Here's an alternative approach for filtering:
-set big_nums = numbers.where(n => n > 5)
-set small_nums = numbers.where(n => n <= 5)
-set a_names = users.where(u => u.starts_with("a"))
+big_nums = numbers.where(n => n > 5)
+small_nums = numbers.where(n => n <= 5)
+a_names = users.where(u => u.starts_with("a"))
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -133,7 +131,7 @@ set a_names = users.where(u => u.starts_with("a"))
 }
 ```
 
-### Sort lists
+## Sort lists
 
 Order elements with [`sort()`](/reference/functions/sort):
 
@@ -142,10 +140,8 @@ from {
   numbers: [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9],
   words: ["zebra", "apple", "banana"]
 }
-set sorted_nums = numbers.sort()
-set sorted_words = words.sort()
-// Note: reverse() is only for strings, not lists
-// To reverse a list, you would need to use the reverse operator in a pipeline
+sorted_nums = numbers.sort()
+sorted_words = words.sort()
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -157,188 +153,195 @@ set sorted_words = words.sort()
 }
 ```
 
-### Get unique values
+Sort in descending order with `desc`:
 
-Remove duplicates with [`distinct()`](/reference/functions/distinct):
+```tql
+from {numbers: [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9]}
+descending = numbers.sort(desc=true)
+```
+
+```tql
+{numbers: [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9], descending: [9, 5, 4, 3, 1, 1]}
+```
+
+Use a custom comparator to sort records by a specific field:
 
 ```tql
 from {
-  items: ["a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "d"],
-  numbers: [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4]
+  users: [
+    {name: "Charlie", age: 35},
+    {name: "Alice", age: 30},
+    {name: "Bob", age: 25}
+  ]
 }
-set unique_items = distinct(items)
-set unique_nums = distinct(numbers)
+by_age = users.sort(cmp=(a, b) => a.age < b.age)
+by_name = users.sort(cmp=(a, b) => a.name < b.name)
 ```
 
 ```tql
 {
-  items: ["a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "d"],
-  numbers: [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4],
-  unique_items: ["a", "b", "c", "d"],
-  unique_nums: [1, 2, 3, 4]
+  users: [
+    {name: "Charlie", age: 35},
+    {name: "Alice", age: 30},
+    {name: "Bob", age: 25}
+  ],
+  by_age: [
+    {name: "Bob", age: 25},
+    {name: "Alice", age: 30},
+    {name: "Charlie", age: 35}
+  ],
+  by_name: [
+    {name: "Alice", age: 30},
+    {name: "Bob", age: 25},
+    {name: "Charlie", age: 35}
+  ]
 }
 ```
 
-### Flatten nested lists
+## Slice lists
 
-Note: Direct list flattening is not currently supported in TQL. The [`flatten()`](/reference/functions/flatten) function is designed for flattening records, not lists. To work with nested lists, you would need to process them element by element.
+Extract portions of a list with [`slice()`](/reference/functions/slice):
 
-## Work with records
+Get the first N elements:
 
-Records (objects) contain key-value pairs. Here's how to manipulate them.
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]}
+first_three = xs.slice(end=3)
+```
 
-### Access record fields
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], first_three: [1, 2, 3]}
+```
 
-Get values using dot notation or brackets:
+Get the last N elements:
 
 ```tql
-from {
-  user: {
-    name: "Alice",
-    age: 30,
-    address: {
-      city: "NYC",
-      zip: "10001"
-    }
-  }
-}
-set name = user.name
-set city = user.address.city
-set zip = user["address"]["zip"]
-set has_email = user.has("email")
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]}
+last_three = xs.slice(begin=-3)
 ```
 
 ```tql
-{
-  user: {
-    name: "Alice",
-    age: 30,
-    address: {city: "NYC", zip: "10001"}
-  },
-  name: "Alice",
-  city: "NYC",
-  zip: "10001",
-  has_email: false
-}
+{xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], last_three: [3, 4, 5]}
 ```
 
-### Get keys and values
+Extract a range:
 
-Extract field names and values:
+```tql
+from {xs: [10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70]}
+middle = xs.slice(begin=2, end=5)
+```
 
 ```tql
-from {
-  config: {
-    host: "localhost",
-    port: 8080,
-    ssl: true
-  }
-}
-set field_names = config.keys()
-// Note: values() function is not available
-set num_fields = config.keys().length()
+{xs: [10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70], middle: [30, 40, 50]}
 ```
 
+Reverse a list:
+
 ```tql
-{
-  config: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
-  field_names: ["host", "port", "ssl"],
-  num_fields: 3
-}
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]}
+reversed = xs.slice(stride=-1)
 ```
 
-### Merge records
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], reversed: [5, 4, 3, 2, 1]}
+```
 
-Combine multiple records with [`merge()`](/reference/functions/merge) or spread syntax:
+Select every Nth element:
 
 ```tql
-from {
-  defaults: {host: "localhost", port: 80, ssl: false},
-  custom: {port: 8080, ssl: true}
-}
-set merged = merge(defaults, custom)
-set spread = {...defaults, ...custom}
-set with_extra = {...defaults, ...custom, debug: true}
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]}
+every_other = xs.slice(stride=2)
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8], every_other: [1, 3, 5, 7]}
+```
+
+## Top-k and bottom-k
+
+Chain [`sort()`](/reference/functions/sort) and [`slice()`](/reference/functions/slice)
+to implement a top-k query over list elements:
+
+```tql
+from {scores: [72, 95, 88, 61, 83, 97, 56]}
+top_3 = scores.sort(desc=true).slice(end=3)
+bottom_3 = scores.sort().slice(end=3)
 ```
 
 ```tql
 {
-  defaults: {host: "localhost", port: 80, ssl: false},
-  custom: {port: 8080, ssl: true},
-  merged: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
-  spread: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
-  with_extra: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true, debug: true}
+  scores: [72, 95, 88, 61, 83, 97, 56],
+  top_3: [97, 95, 88],
+  bottom_3: [56, 61, 72]
 }
 ```
 
-### Transform record values
+## Get unique values
 
-Note: The `map_values` function is not available in TQL. To transform record values, you would need to reconstruct the record with transformed values manually:
+Remove duplicates with [`distinct()`](/reference/functions/distinct):
 
 ```tql
 from {
-  prices: {
-    apple: 1.50,
-    banana: 0.75,
-    orange: 2.00
-  }
+  items: ["a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "d"],
+  numbers: [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4]
 }
-// Manual transformation example:
-set with_tax = {
-  apple: prices.apple * 1.1,
-  banana: prices.banana * 1.1,
-  orange: prices.orange * 1.1
+unique_items = distinct(items).sort()
+unique_nums = distinct(numbers).sort()
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  items: ["a", "b", "a", "c", "b", "d"],
+  numbers: [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4],
+  unique_items: ["a", "b", "c", "d"],
+  unique_nums: [1, 2, 3, 4]
 }
 ```
 
-### Filter record fields
+## Use lists as sets
 
-Keep only specific fields:
+Use [`add()`](/reference/functions/add) for set-insertion (append only if absent)
+and [`remove()`](/reference/functions/remove) to delete all occurrences:
 
 ```tql
-from {
-  user: {
-    id: 123,
-    name: "Alice",
-    email: "alice@example.com",
-    password: "secret",
-    api_key: "xyz123"
-  }
-}
-// Note: filter_keys and select functions are not available
-// Manual field selection:
-set public_info = {
-  id: user.id,
-  name: user.name,
-  email: user.email
-}
-set contact = {
-  name: user.name,
-  email: user.email
+from {tags: ["info", "warning", "info"]}
+with_error = tags.add("error")
+still_same = tags.add("info")
+without_info = tags.remove("info")
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  tags: ["info", "warning", "info"],
+  with_error: ["info", "warning", "info", "error"],
+  still_same: ["info", "warning", "info"],
+  without_info: ["warning"]
 }
 ```
 
+Combine `add()` with [`distinct()`](/reference/functions/distinct) when you need
+a deduplicated collection:
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 2, 3]}
+unique = distinct(xs).sort()
+with_4 = unique.add(4)
+with_2 = unique.add(2)
+```
+
 ```tql
 {
-  user: {
-    id: 123,
-    name: "Alice",
-    email: "alice@example.com",
-    password: "secret",
-    api_key: "xyz123"
-  },
-  public_info: {
-    id: 123,
-    name: "Alice",
-    email: "alice@example.com"
-  },
-  contact: {
-    name: "Alice",
-    email: "alice@example.com"
-  }
+  xs: [1, 2, 2, 3],
+  unique: [1, 2, 3],
+  with_4: [1, 2, 3, 4],
+  with_2: [1, 2, 3]
 }
 ```
 
+## Flatten nested lists
+
+Note: Direct list flattening is not currently supported in TQL. The [`flatten()`](/reference/functions/flatten) function is designed for flattening records, not lists. To work with nested lists, you would need to process them element by element.
+
 ## Combine lists and records
 
 Work with collections of records:
@@ -351,9 +354,9 @@ from {
     {name: "Charlie", age: 35, city: "NYC"}
   ]
 }
-set names = users.map(u => u.name)
-set nyc_users = users.where(u => u.city == "NYC")
-set avg_age = users.map(u => u.age).sum() / users.length()
+names = users.map(u => u.name)
+nyc_users = users.where(u => u.city == "NYC")
+avg_age = users.map(u => u.age).sum() / users.length()
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -385,9 +388,9 @@ from {
   cities: ["NYC", "SF", "LA"]
 }
 // Note: zip only takes 2 arguments, returns records with left/right fields
-set name_age = zip(names, ages)
-set zipped = zip(name_age, cities)
-set users = zipped.map(z => {
+name_age = zip(names, ages)
+zipped = zip(name_age, cities)
+users = zipped.map(z => {
   name: z.left.left,
   age: z.left.right,
   city: z.right
@@ -471,7 +474,7 @@ san_entries = domains.map(name => {
     {
       name: "example.com",
       type: "DNSName",
-      verified: true
+      verified: false
     },
     {
       name: "www.example.com",
@@ -526,8 +529,8 @@ from {
     }
   }
 }
-set users = response.data.users
-set summaries = users.map(u, {
+users = response.data.users
+summaries = users.map(u => {
   name: u.name,
   avg_score: u.scores.sum() / u.scores.length(),
   max_score: u.scores.max()
@@ -560,10 +563,10 @@ from {
     {id: "C003", name: "Tool", price: 15}
   ]
 }
-set first_item = items.first()
-set ids = items.map(item => item.id)
-set names = items.map(item => item.name)
-set expensive = items.where(item => item.price > 15)
+first_item = items.first()
+ids = items.map(item => item.id)
+names = items.map(item => item.name)
+expensive = items.where(item => item.price > 15)
 ```
 
 ```tql
@@ -586,10 +589,11 @@ set expensive = items.where(item => item.price > 15)
 4. **Handle empty collections**: Check length before accessing elements
 5. **Preserve immutability**: Collection functions return new values, not modify existing
 
-import Guide from "@components/see-also/Guide.astro";
-
-## See also
+## See Also
 
+- <Fn>sort</Fn>
+- <Fn>slice</Fn>
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-records</Guide>

@@ -0,0 +1,168 @@
+---
+title: Shape records
+---
+
+import Fn from "@components/see-also/Fn.astro";
+import Guide from "@components/see-also/Guide.astro";
+
+Records (objects) contain key-value pairs. This guide shows you how to work with
+records — accessing fields, extracting keys, merging, and transforming values.
+
+## Access record fields
+
+Get values using dot notation or brackets:
+
+```tql
+from {
+  user: {
+    name: "Alice",
+    age: 30,
+    address: {
+      city: "NYC",
+      zip: "10001"
+    }
+  }
+}
+name = user.name
+city = user.address.city
+zip = user["address"]["zip"]
+has_email = user.has("email")
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  user: {
+    name: "Alice",
+    age: 30,
+    address: {city: "NYC", zip: "10001"}
+  },
+  name: "Alice",
+  city: "NYC",
+  zip: "10001",
+  has_email: false
+}
+```
+
+## Get keys and values
+
+Extract field names and values:
+
+```tql
+from {
+  config: {
+    host: "localhost",
+    port: 8080,
+    ssl: true
+  }
+}
+field_names = config.keys()
+// Note: values() function is not available
+num_fields = config.keys().length()
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  config: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
+  field_names: ["host", "port", "ssl"],
+  num_fields: 3
+}
+```
+
+## Merge records
+
+Combine multiple records with [`merge()`](/reference/functions/merge) or spread syntax:
+
+```tql
+from {
+  defaults: {host: "localhost", port: 80, ssl: false},
+  custom: {port: 8080, ssl: true}
+}
+merged = merge(defaults, custom)
+spread = {...defaults, ...custom}
+with_extra = {...defaults, ...custom, debug: true}
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  defaults: {host: "localhost", port: 80, ssl: false},
+  custom: {port: 8080, ssl: true},
+  merged: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
+  spread: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true},
+  with_extra: {host: "localhost", port: 8080, ssl: true, debug: true}
+}
+```
+
+## Transform record values
+
+Note: The `map_values` function is not available in TQL. To transform record values, you would need to reconstruct the record with transformed values manually:
+
+```tql
+from {
+  prices: {
+    apple: 1.50,
+    banana: 0.75,
+    orange: 2.00
+  }
+}
+// Manual transformation example:
+with_tax = {
+  apple: prices.apple * 1.1,
+  banana: prices.banana * 1.1,
+  orange: prices.orange * 1.1
+}
+```
+
+## Filter record fields
+
+Keep only specific fields:
+
+```tql
+from {
+  user: {
+    id: 123,
+    name: "Alice",
+    email: "alice@example.com",
+    password: "secret",
+    api_key: "xyz123"
+  }
+}
+// Note: filter_keys and select functions are not available
+// Manual field selection:
+public_info = {
+  id: user.id,
+  name: user.name,
+  email: user.email
+}
+contact = {
+  name: user.name,
+  email: user.email
+}
+```
+
+```tql
+{
+  user: {
+    id: 123,
+    name: "Alice",
+    email: "alice@example.com",
+    password: "secret",
+    api_key: "xyz123"
+  },
+  public_info: {
+    id: 123,
+    name: "Alice",
+    email: "alice@example.com"
+  },
+  contact: {
+    name: "Alice",
+    email: "alice@example.com"
+  }
+}
+```
+
+## See Also
+
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists</Guide>
+- <Guide>transformation/filter-and-select-data</Guide>
+- <Guide>transformation/transform-values</Guide>
+- <Guide>transformation/reshape-complex-data</Guide>

@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@ example: "xs.add(y)"
 ---
 
 import Fn from '@components/see-also/Fn.astro';
+import Guide from '@components/see-also/Guide.astro';
 
 Adds an element into a list if it doesn't already exist (set-insertion).
 
@@ -68,3 +69,4 @@ xs = xs.add("hello")
 - <Fn>distinct</Fn>
 - <Fn>prepend</Fn>
 - <Fn>remove</Fn>
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists</Guide>

@@ -75,4 +75,4 @@ select x = this.get("x", -1), y = this.get("y", -1)
 ## See Also
 
 - <Fn>keys</Fn>
-- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists-and-records</Guide>
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists</Guide>

@@ -81,4 +81,4 @@ select result = merge(x, y)
 ## See Also
 
 - <Fn>concatenate</Fn>
-- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists-and-records</Guide>
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-records</Guide>

@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@ example: "xs.remove(y)"
 ---

+import Guide from '@components/see-also/Guide.astro';
 
 Removes all occurrences of an element from a list.
 
@@ -66,3 +67,4 @@ xs = xs.remove("apple")

@@ -4,6 +4,9 @@ category: List, Record
 example: 'xs.sort()'
 ---
 
+import Fn from '@components/see-also/Fn.astro';
+import Guide from '@components/see-also/Guide.astro';
+
 Sorts lists and record fields.
 
 ```tql
@@ -86,3 +89,8 @@ xs = xs.sort(cmp=(a, b) => a.v < b.v)
 ```tql
 {xs: [{v: 1, id: "a"}, {v: 2, id: "b"}, {v: 2, id: "c"}]}
 ```
+
+## See Also
+
+- <Fn>slice</Fn>
+- <Guide>transformation/shape-lists</Guide>
